ceilingRelativePoint.description: usually, the ceiling portal's relative point is the centre of the room. However, in cases where multi-rooms are stitched together into a single room, this relationship is broken. Ie, bookworld28/ bookworld29. In this case, this point is used instead

color.description: the color the room was shown in in the zx spectrum original game. This is used to provide highlight colours in each room

height.description: custom room height that can be set per-room in blocks. If not set, the default room height is used. Only a few of the original game rooms need this to make the transition to the next room above work better. Can be used to fine-tune the point in a jump where the room above loads.

direction.description: this wall would normally be found on the (invisible) towards side of the room
this wall would normally be found/rendered on the away side of the room

tiles.description: the tiles to show - the length of this array also determines the size of the wall

direction.description: this wall would normally be found/rendered on the left side of the room

tiles.description: the tiles to show - the length of this array also determines the size of the wall

was.description: it is the bubbles that play the sound when something is fading out, not the item itself. This property helps us know which sound to play

speed.description: speed multiplier — undefined is treated as 1 (original game speed)

toDoor.description: the id of the door in the destination room. This usually does not need to be given since the game can choose the door facing the right way from the destination room. only give this if there are multiple doors in the same direction between the two rooms

type.description: this switch targets the redux store

activates.description: true is a shorthand for monsters/platforms that are activated by default:   {leftState: {activated: true, everActivated:true}, rightState: {activated:false}}, false is shorthand for monsters/platforms that are deactivated by default:   {leftState: {activated: false}, rightState: {activated: true, everActivated:true}},

switchedDirection.description: shortcut - gives this direction for left state and opposite direction for right state

flip.description: this switch will flip the other switch when it is flipped

makesStable.description: if true, equivalent to leftState disappearing on stand, right state not disappearing if false, equivalent to leftState not disappearing, right state disappearing on stand

activates.description: true is a shorthand for charles bots that are activated by default:   {leftState: {activated: true}, rightState: {activated: false}}, false is shorthand for charles bots that are deactivated by default:   {leftState: {activated: false}, rightState: {activated: true}},

type.description: this switch targets items in the room

delay.description: how long to delay until the first emitting? after this time the first emit will happen, then all others will continue at the period interval. undefined is treated the same as 0

emits.description: what does this emitter emit? Could be (potentially) any free item

maximumAtOnce.description: How many items emitted from this emitter can be in the room at once? If undefined, no limit. If already this many items in the room, the items will have to be removed from the room before more can be emitted (for example, collecting an emitted pickup)

activates.description: true is a shorthand for monsters/platforms that are activated by default:   {leftState: {activated: true, everActivated:true}, rightState: {activated:false}}, false is shorthand for monsters/platforms that are deactivated by default:   {leftState: {activated: false}, rightState: {activated: true, everActivated:true}},

switchedDirection.description: shortcut - gives this direction for left state and opposite direction for right state

flip.description: this switch will flip the other switch when it is flipped

makesStable.description: if true, equivalent to leftState disappearing on stand, right state not disappearing if false, equivalent to leftState not disappearing, right state disappearing on stand

activates.description: true is a shorthand for charles bots that are activated by default:   {leftState: {activated: true}, rightState: {activated: false}}, false is shorthand for charles bots that are deactivated by default:   {leftState: {activated: false}, rightState: {activated: true}},

floorType.description: the room has no floor, but it is included to draw the floor edge

items.description: by keying each item with an id, it makes the diffing easier since the array is no longer position-dependent

nonContiguousRelationship.description: for rooms that are shown on the same map even though they don't have any physical connection

gridOffset.description: the other room needs to have the opposite (* -1) of this

gridPosition.description: the grid position (on the map) of this sub-room

physicalPosition.description: where the sub-room actually starts and ends once loaded (so we can work out which sub-room items are in while the game is in-play)

subRooms.description: subRooms are used for the map for rooms which were modelled as multiple rooms in the original game

delay.description: how long to delay until the first emitting? after this time the first emit will happen, then all others will continue at the period interval. undefined is treated the same as 0

maximumAtOnce.description: How many items emitted from this emitter can be in the room at once? If undefined, no limit. If already this many items in the room, the items will have to be removed from the room before more can be emitted (for example, collecting an emitted pickup)

r2.description: what does this emitter emit? Could be (potentially) any free item

toRoom.description: note that if the other room contains exactly one teleporter, we need not give the position or the item If undefined, is a same-room teleporter

toItemId.description:
an item in the destination room this teleporter should go to - the player will be moved to atop this item

If not given, will find the (only teleporter) in the destination room

note: not RoomItemId because that is the ids of items in *this* room, but this is pointing to another room

toRoom.description: note that if the other room contains exactly one teleporter, we need not give the position or the item.

r8.description: if given, the item disappears after the specified interaction. This must be null (not undefined) so switches can tell the difference between having no setting, and having a setting to change to null when they make something not disappearing

r11.description: if given, the item will disappear only if stood/touched by items of this type. Eg, set to ['head', 'heels', 'headOverHeels'] to make only when touched by the player or ['head'] eg for doughnuts that only head can collect

r19.description:
The item will be removed from the room after the room it is in has more than this roomTime. To guarantee removal on the next frame (effectively immediately) set to -1. Otherwise, can set to the current roomTime + duration of an animation that needs to play

If null, the item is not scheduled for removal (the normal case)

r20.description:
where in the destination room this teleporter should go - usually to atop another teleporter, but could be anywhere.

If not given, will find the (only teleporter) in the destination room

r42.description: activated for us is a boolean, not the many-states from the json config, ie it is stateful on if the item is currently activated (so they can render differently)

r45.description: if this item has ever been activated, in the lifetime of the room. Charging cybermen will have this flag as false so long as they are charging

r60.description: how many total should this emitter emit? Null for no limit
